---
title: "About"
about:
  template: trestles
---

This blog is a byproduct of studying neuroradiology. I'm a radiologist working through the literature systematically — mostly review articles from RadioGraphics, AJNR, and AJR — and writing up what I learn as a way of consolidating it.

I’m a young Padawan in the world of neuroradiology, and these posts are my notes, rewritten into something readable enough to be worth coming back to. The format is deliberately mechanistic: I find that understanding why an imaging finding looks the way it does is more durable than memorizing what it looks like. Most posts end up being as much about pathophysiology as about sequences and signal characteristics.

---

**A note on accuracy**

The posts are grounded in peer-reviewed literature — each one is based on a specific review article, linked at the top. But this is still a personal learning blog, not a clinical reference, and my interpretations may be incomplete or occasionally wrong. The literature moves, criteria get revised, and I am not always the last to know.

If you're using any of this for patient care or exam preparation: the primary source is linked, and that's the thing you should actually trust. 

---

**Contact**

Questions, corrections, and suggestions are welcome at [c.kurmann.ck@gmail.com](mailto:c.kurmann.ck@gmail.com).
